findings.description: The signature placement differs between PDF and HTML. In the PDF, the signature appears inline next to the From field. In the HTML, the signature appears below the From block.
findings.evidence: PDF: signature is positioned to the right of the From: Ed Mussler / Green Square Central Office / Division of Waste Management block. HTML: signature appears as a separate block below the From information.

findings.description: The report outline list uses different formatting. PDF uses Roman numeral indentation with tab-aligned content, while HTML uses a simpler left-aligned list.
findings.evidence: PDF: Roman numerals I-IV with tab-indented descriptions. HTML: Similar but with less indentation structure.

findings.description: The oral comments summary topics use underlined bold formatting in the PDF. The HTML preserves the underline and bold but bullet style differs slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF uses traditional bullet points with underlined bold topic headings. HTML uses disc-style bullets with underlined bold headings - functionally equivalent.

findings.description: The Attachments list in Section IV uses Roman numeral formatting with different indentation in HTML vs PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: Roman numerals I-IX with center-aligned numbering and tab-indented content. HTML: Roman numerals with left-aligned simpler formatting.

findings.description: The document uses 'MEMORANDUM' as h1 and subsequent sections I-IV as h2, but the memo title line is styled as h1 with underline. The actual document title should arguably be the subject line or the memorandum heading. Additionally, the main content sections use multiple <main> elements which is semantically incorrect HTML but not a heading hierarchy issue per se. The heading hierarchy itself (h1 for MEMORANDUM, h2 for sections I-IV) is reasonable and consistent.
findings.evidence: h1: MEMORANDUM, h2: I. History/Background, h2: II. March 1, 2016 Public Meeting..., h2: III. Written Public Comment Summary, h2: IV. Attachments — this is acceptable.

findings.description: The footer content from page 1 (State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management, address, phone number) is duplicated — it appears both in the HTML header area and again in a page-footer div. The PDF shows this as the letterhead header at the top and a footer bar at the bottom of page 1, so some duplication exists but is minor structural repetition from the original.
findings.evidence: Header: 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management 1646 Mail Service Center | 217 West Jones Street | Raleigh, 27699-1646 919 707 8200' appears in both the outer page structure and in a page-footer div.

findings.description: Alt text for images is reasonable. The logo alt text ('North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality Waste Management logo'), signature alt text ('Signature of Ed Mussler'), and footer bar alt text ('Blue decorative footer bar') are descriptive and appropriate. These were not present in the PDF and represent good additions.
findings.evidence: alt='North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality Waste Management logo', alt='Signature of Ed Mussler', alt='Blue decorative footer bar'
